content: Third-party embeddable JS SDKs (Intercom/Drift/chat widgets/analytics tags) follow a single-script-tag pattern: a small bootstrap loader reads config from data-* attributes or document.currentScript, then the SDK self-initializes. Internally they separate concerns into distinct modules: event capture, a data layer/message bus, and a rendering layer, communicating via an internal pub/sub rather than direct calls so capture and render stay decoupled. Style isolation for the rendered UI is done via Shadow DOM (preferred over iframe for same-origin overlays needing to inherit host fonts) or scoped CSS. No official single 'architecture' spec exists; this is convergent industry practice across analytics/chat/tag-manager tooling.
